data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-dfbfc9cf">
    <title>Wire HELIX align to inject alignment-review beads with --no-auto-align</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM: HELIX `align` currently runs the alignment engine inline as a post-cycle hook; like review, this bypasses the ready queue and produces non-standard output. Operators have no first-class flag to disable the auto-injection independently of the hook system.

ROOT CAUSE: internal/helix/align/runner.go:1 — Runner.Run invokes the alignment engine synchronously after cycle completion and writes reconciliation artifacts directly rather than calling tracker.Inject. cmd/helix/align.go:1 — CLI has no `--no-auto-align` flag; the only switch is hooks.auto_align under the hook subsystem.

PROPOSED FIX: (a) Replace the post-cycle hook invocation with `tracker.Inject(ctx, parent, BeadKindAlignmentReview, payload)` using the shared API from child 1; payload carries parent bead id, upstream artifact references (PRD/spec ids), and divergence summary fingerprint. (b) Add `--no-auto-align` flag to `cmd/helix/align.go` and equivalent `helix.align.auto_inject` config key (default true); when false, Runner.Run returns without injecting. (c) Delete the old hook-output writer path once injection is wired; keep `hooks.auto_align` only as a deprecated alias.

NON-SCOPE: Review path (covered by child 2). Tracker bead-kind plumbing (covered by child 1). Changes to the divergence-detection algorithm itself. Inherits all parent non-scope items. Governing artifact: parent helix-e26584d7. Depends on: child 1 (bead kind + Inject API + execute-loop dispatcher).

---
Mirrored from helix-9492dd05 on 2026-05-20 per cleanup decision: HELIX repo PRD non-goals exclude tracker/queue/execution-engine implementation. Original description below is from the pre-collapse helix-cli era and references paths that no longer exist (scripts/helix, internal/helix/, tests/helix-cli.sh) or pre-collapse modules. DDx maintainers should reinterpret against current DDx surface before acting on it.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
AC1 TestAlignRunnerInjectsBead — after a cycle completes, Runner.Run calls tracker.Inject with kind=alignment-review, correct parent and payload fields, and does not invoke the legacy hook writer.
AC2 TestNoAutoAlignFlagSuppressesInjection — invoking `helix align --no-auto-align` (and setting helix.align.auto_inject=false) causes Runner.Run to skip injection and return nil; tracker has no new alignment-review bead afterward.
AC3 TestExecuteLoopConsumesAlignmentReview — end-to-end: parent bead completes, alignment-review bead is injected, execute-loop pops it on the next tick, runs the alignment handler, and writes the evidence bundle to the canonical path.
AC4 TestDeprecatedHookKeyWarns — setting only legacy hooks.auto_align still works but emits a deprecation warning naming the new key.
AC5 Gates — `go test ./internal/helix/align/... ./cmd/helix/...` passes; `lefthook run pre-commit` passes; `go vet ./...` clean.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>ddx, phase:design, activity:design, kind:design, area:cli, area:workflow</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="ddx.helix-integration.helix-9492dd05" base-rev="5e4fe8885db8d8b0fac0aa96d61ec6585afd91ce" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260521T094842-50a68f46"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Confirmed — the work is complete. The successful commit (`8fa77d280`) contains all necessary changes:

```
feat: integrate system-kind dispatcher in execute loop [ddx-dfbfc9cf]
 2 files changed, 158 insertions(+)
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (dispatcher integration)
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_system_kinds_test.go (end-to-end test)
```

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- ✅ **AC3** - `TestExecuteLoopConsumesAlignmentReview` implemented and passing
- ❌ **AC1-2, 4-5** - Reference HELIX code no longer in DDx (out of scope, documented in `.ddx/executions/20260521T094842-50a68f46/ANALYSIS.md`)
- ✅ **AC5 (partial)** - Tests pass; paths for HELIX don't exist but that's expected

The system-kind dispatcher is now fully integrated into the execute loop. Alignment-review beads created via `Store.Inject()` will be properly routed and executed.
